differential_diagnoses: Echis ocellatus envenomation with venom-induced consumption coagulopathy (VICC)
Acute kidney injury (likely acute tubular or cortical necrosis secondary to shock and venom-induced nephrotoxicity)
next_steps: Immediate administration of appropriate polyvalent antivenom
Aggressive fluid resuscitation with monitoring for fluid overload
Correction of coagulopathy with fresh whole blood or fresh frozen plasma and platelets
Urgent obstetric consultation for management of placental abruption and delivery
Removal of tourniquet under medical supervision following antivenom administration
Emergent transfer to a tertiary facility with specialized obstetric and nephrological care
Serial monitoring of hematological, renal, and coagulation profiles
